description: Modifying a collection while iterating it with a fail-fast iterator, throwing ConcurrentModificationException.
body: Failure mode: code calls list.remove/add inside an enhanced-for loop over the same collection, so the fail-fast iterator detects the structural change and throws ConcurrentModificationException (or worse, silently corrupts under concurrency). Detection signals: ConcurrentModificationException stack trace pointing at the iterator's next(); the bug appears only when an element is removed; intermittent under multiple threads. Do not structurally modify a collection during its own iteration. Safer fallback: use Iterator.remove(), collect-then-remove with removeIf, iterate a copy, or use a concurrent collection (CopyOnWriteArrayList / ConcurrentHashMap) for multi-threaded access; verify with a test that removes a matching element.
